title: Images, video, embedding
assignment: Review the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: The image and its voice
groups.0.items.0.def: is void — no . src says which image, alt says what IS SEEN on it. For a blind person the image IS this text, there is no other. Do not write "photo" (they already know) and do not write the filename.
groups.0.items.1.def: alt="" — deliberately empty, for pure decoration: a divider, a background, an icon next to text that already says the same. It tells the reader "skip me". A deliberately empty alt and a missing alt are different things — the difference is huge.
groups.0.items.2.def: src is the same path as with links. C:\Users\… works only on your machine. And on a server Bike.jpg and bike.jpg are different files — hence "works on my machine".
groups.1.title: Video, sound, icon
groups.1.items.0.def: — it has a closing tag, unlike , because things live inside: fallback text, captions ( — the deaf person's only way), other formats. Without controls it is a still frame. autoplay — never: the person just closes the tab.
groups.1.items.1.def: <link rel="icon" href="..."> in <head>. A void tag. Without it the tab is a blank sheet — the site looks unfinished and the person cannot find it again among twenty other tabs.
groups.2.title: Someone else's page inside
groups.2.items.0.def: <iframe> is a window into a whole external site — maps, video, payment. A wall divides them: you do not touch it, it does not touch you. title says what is inside — a blind person otherwise hears nothing and does not know whether to enter. (Not the <title> from the head — the names just match.) On untrusted code, add sandbox.
